top_reddit.title: Is the ""digital nomad"" dream just a fancy way of being broke abroad?
top_reddit.snippet:
I've been following this sub for a while and watching a lot of YouTube channels about digital nomads in places like Bali, Chiang Mai, and Medellín. On the surface, it all looks amazing — laptops on the beach, smoothies for breakfast, cheap rent, flexible schedules.

But the more I dig into it, the more it seems like a lot of people are just scrapin
top_reddit.author: No_Lavishness2922

top_reddit.title: I cut my cost of living by 70% by moving to Vietnam. Here's exactly where the money goes.
top_reddit.snippet:
34M, Australian, been working remotely from Southeast Asia for the past 2 years. 18 months in Thailand then 6 months in Vietnam. Currently still in Vietnam and honestly not sure I want to go back.
I used to spend around $3,500 USD a month back in Australia just existing. Rent, food, transport, health stuff, going out occasionally. Nothing crazy. No
top_reddit.author: pegasus7x77
